QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_24

question.text: Mr. Vikram gifted mutual fund units worth Rs.3,00,000 to his wife Shalini. In the same year, the mutual fund incurred a loss of Rs.25,000. Which of the following statements is correct regarding this loss?

answer.text: The loss belongs to Shalini as the legal owner; no clubbing for losses
The loss is completely disallowed for both Vikram and Shalini
The loss of Rs.25,000 is clubbed with Vikram's income, as clubbing provisions apply to both income and losses from transferred assets
The loss is split equally between Vikram and Shalini (Rs.12,500 each)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Clubbing provisions under Section 64 apply to INCOME from transferred assets. If the net result is a LOSS, the loss is also clubbed with the transferor. So the Rs.25,000 loss from the mutual fund units gifted to Shalini will be included in Vikram's computation — he can use this loss for set-off against his other income as per applicable rules. **The Trap:** Option A assumes clubbing applies only to positive income, not losses. This is incorrect — clubbing is about attributing the income/loss to the right person, regardless of sign. **Cross-Exam Context:** The "clubbing of losses" concept is a nuanced point tested in NISM X-B to differentiate from basic X-A questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clubbing of losses can actually benefit the transferor if they have income to set off against. However, this should never be the primary motive for gifting assets.
